description_long: This is a stone obelisk monument with a square base composed of multiple stepped layers, giving it a solid and elevated appearance. The shaft rises vertically from the base and is adorned with classical architectural details, including triangular pediments on each face just below the cap. The cap itself is ornate, featuring a decorative urn or finial at the very top, which adds a sense of grandeur. The surfaces of the obelisk show weathering and subtle color variations, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. Some faces appear to have panels that may once have held inscriptions or plaques, though details are worn and not clearly legible. The overall style is reminiscent of Victorian or neoclassical memorials commonly found in parks, plazas, or cemeteries.
description: A weathered stone obelisk monument with a stepped square base, classical pediments, and an ornate urn finial at the top. The surfaces show age and subtle color variation, with decorative architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main face with a prominent triangular pediment and the urn finial clearly visible atop the cap.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the right side, highlighting the stepped base and the depth of the architectural details.
description_view_2: Rear-side view with the urn finial still visible, showing a less detailed face and more weathering on the lower base.
description_view_3: Left-side view emphasizing the layered base and the urn finial, with some erosion and discoloration visible on the lower platform.
attribute_ornamentation: Decorative triangular pediments on each face below the cap, and an ornate urn finial at the tip. Subtle reliefs and classical motifs are present on the upper section.
attribute_base_design: The base is a multi-stepped square platform with beveled edges, constructed from large stone blocks. The steps are broad and provide a stable foundation, with some weathering and discoloration visible.
attribute_tip_design: The tip features a decorative urn or finial with a rounded, fluted design, sitting atop a square capstone. The urn is detailed and contrasts with the angular lines of the shaft.
